sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content:
Primary treatment for ovarian cancer includes surgical staging and cytoreduction. Surgery is also used for interval debulking after neoadjuvant chemotherapy and for secondary cytoreduction in recurrent disease.

Principles:
- An open laparotomy with vertical midline incision is standard for most patients. Minimally invasive techniques may be used by experienced surgeons for early-stage disease or interval debulking.
- Goal is maximal cytoreduction: complete resection (R0) or optimal residual disease (<1 cm).
- Comprehensive staging includes peritoneal cytology, peritoneal biopsies, omentectomy, para-aortic and pelvic lymph node dissection, and resection of all visible disease.
- Fertility-sparing surgery (USO with uterus preservation) may be considered for select patients with apparent early-stage disease and low-risk histologies who desire fertility.
- Special considerations for mucinous tumors (GI evaluation), borderline tumors (consider omentectomy and peritoneal biopsies), and LCOC (individualized approach).

Resectability Criteria: Optimal cytoreduction is defined as residual disease <1 cm. Complete resection (R0) is the goal. Factors affecting resectability include disease distribution, patient performance status, and surgeon experience. Laparoscopic assessment may predict ability to achieve optimal cytoreduction.

Pre-operative Optimisation: Assess nutritional status, performance status, and comorbidities. Correct anemia, electrolyte imbalances. Consider ERAS protocols to reduce length of stay. Counsel patients about potential for IP chemotherapy and need for port placement.

cards.name: Primary Cytoreductive Surgery (PCS)
cards.detail:
Approach: Open laparotomy; minimally invasive for select early-stage or interval debulking.
Indications: Newly diagnosed ovarian cancer when optimal cytoreduction is feasible.
Key Complications: Bowel resection, splenectomy, diaphragm stripping, hemorrhage, infection, urinary tract injury.
Operative Mortality: Low (<1%) in high-volume centers; higher with extensive upper abdominal surgery.

cards.name: Interval Debulking Surgery (IDS)
cards.detail:
Approach: Open or minimally invasive after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Indications: Patients with advanced disease who received NACT and achieved response or stable disease.
Key Complications: Similar to PCS; may consider HIPEC with cisplatin for stage III disease.
Operative Mortality: Similar to PCS.

cards.name: Secondary Cytoreductive Surgery
cards.detail:
Approach: Open or minimally invasive for isolated recurrent disease.
Indications: Platinum-sensitive recurrence (≥6 months), good performance status, no ascites, resectable disease.
Key Complications: Bowel resection, adhesiolysis, risk of fistula.
Operative Mortality: Low in selected patients.

cards.name: Risk-Reducing Salpingo-Oophorectomy (RRSO)
cards.detail:
Approach: Laparoscopic.
Indications: BRCA1/2 mutation carriers for cancer prevention.
Key Complications: Surgical complications; menopause induction.
Operative Mortality: Very low.
